monitor-blue.purpose: Distance metrics, primary actions, accent color. The PM5's signature blue.

split-orange.purpose: Watts, speed, split time. The amber of a PM5 split display.

green-zone-green.purpose: Positive deltas, target zone indicators, success states.

red-zone-red.purpose: Heart rate metrics, negative deltas, finish markers.

cadence-violet.purpose: Cadence, stroke rate, secondary rhythm metrics.

duration-teal.purpose: Time and duration metrics. Cool and measured, distinct from Monitor Blue.

hero.purpose: Primary metric value in summary cards. SF Pro Rounded bold, at most once per card.

headline.purpose: Panel titles and section headers. SF Pro semibold.

body.purpose: Supporting text and descriptions. SF Pro regular, max 65-75ch line length.

metric.purpose: Data values in badges and tiles. SF Pro semibold with monospacedDigit.

label.purpose: Labels beneath metric values. SF Pro medium, secondary text color.

compact.purpose: Tight spaces: sidebar badges, comparison deltas, timestamps.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default macOS easing for state transitions and navigation.

components.name: Metric Tile
components.description: Dashboard summary card displaying one headline metric with icon and label.
components.html: 42.5 kmDistance
components.css: .ds-metric-tile { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: flex-start; gap: 12px; padding: 16px; border-radius: 8px; background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.7); backdrop-filter: blur(16px); } .ds-metric-tile-value { font-family: 'SF Pro Rounded', system-ui; font-size: 28px; font-weight: 700; color: #0066CC; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-metric-tile-label { font-family: 'SF Pro', system-ui; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 500; color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5); }

components.name: Card Panel
components.description: Chart container with section headline. Used for chart panels in dashboard.
components.html:
Distance by Sport

Chart content
components.css: .ds-card-panel { padding: 16px; border-radius: 8px; background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.03); } .ds-card-panel-title { font-family: 'SF Pro', system-ui; font-size: 15px; font-weight: 600; color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.85); margin: 0 0 12px 0; } .ds-card-panel-content { font-family: 'SF Pro', system-ui; font-size: 13px; color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.6); }

components.name: Personal Best Card
components.description: Material-backed card for personal best entries. Sport icon, distance label, time, pace, date.
components.html:
2k RowErg
7:12.31:48.0/500mJul 2026
components.css: .ds-pb-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; padding: 8px; border-radius: 6px; background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.7); backdrop-filter: blur(16px); } .ds-pb-card-header { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; font-family: 'SF Pro', system-ui; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 500; } .ds-pb-card-label { color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.4); } .ds-pb-card-time { font-family: 'SF Pro', system-ui; font-size: 18px; font-weight: 600; color: #007A99; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-pb-card-pace { font-family: 'SF Pro', system-ui; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 500; color: #0066CC; } .ds-pb-card-date { font-family: 'SF Pro', system-ui; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 500; color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.25); }

components.name: Segmented Picker
components.description: Sport filter segmented control in toolbar. Four segments: All, RowErg, SkiErg, BikeErg.
components.css: .ds-segmented { display: inline-flex; border-radius: 6px; background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.06); padding: 2px; } .ds-segmented-btn { font-family: 'SF Pro', system-ui; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; padding: 4px 12px; border: none; border-radius: 4px; background: transparent; color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.6); cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s, color 0.15s; } .ds-segmented-btn.selected { background: white; color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.85); } .ds-segmented-btn:hover:not(.selected) { background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.04); }

components.name: Empty State
components.description: Actionable centered state shown when no workouts exist in the library.
components.html:
Ready When You Are

Enable Demo Mode to explore sample workouts, or connect your Concept2 logbook in Settings.
components.css: .ds-empty-state { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 12px; padding: 64px 24px; text-align: center; } .ds-empty-title { font-family: 'SF Pro', system-ui; font-size: 17px; font-weight: 600; color: rgba(0,0,0,0.6); margin: 0; } .ds-empty-desc { font-family: 'SF Pro', system-ui; font-size: 13px; color: rgba(0,0,0,0.4); max-width: 320px; margin: 0; }

narrative.overview: RowPlay Studio channels the clarity of the Concept2 PM5 performance monitor — a display designed for one purpose: showing athletes their numbers without distraction. The design is clean and focused, with color serving meaning rather than decoration. Every hue maps to a specific metric role, just as the PM5's LCD uses color sparingly. The system is intentionally flat with tonal layering; it rejects the Strava/Garmin fitness-app aesthetic entirely.
keyCharacteristics: Metric-first: color, typography, and layout serve the numbers
Flat with tonal layering: macOS materials and opacity, no shadows
Semantic color: each color maps to exactly one metric domain
System-native: SF Pro as the single typeface
Quiet energy: color accents are deliberate and sparing

rules.name: The Metric Mapping Rule
rules.body: Each color maps to exactly one metric domain. Never use Split Orange for distance or Monitor Blue for heart rate. The athlete builds muscle memory with these color associations.

rules.name: The One Hero Rule
rules.body: SF Pro Rounded appears on at most one element per card. If every number is rounded, none of them matter.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: No shadows, ever. Depth is tonal, not spatial. If a surface needs to feel elevated, use a more opaque macOS material.

dos: Do use Monitor Blue exclusively for distance, primary actions, and accent color
Do use Split Orange for watts, speed, and split time
Do use SF Pro Rounded only for hero metric values — at most one per card
Do keep surfaces flat with tonal layering via macOS materials and opacity
Do use monospacedDigit() for all numeric metric displays
Do provide accessibility labels and values on every metric tile
donts: Don't use dark or black backgrounds — avoid the Strava/Garmin aesthetic
Don't add social or gamified badge systems, challenge counters, or feed elements
Don't use gradient text, glassmorphism, or decorative blurs
Don't use the hero-metric template as a default card pattern
Don't add shadows to any surface
Don't cross-assign semantic colors between metric domains
